task_completion.description: Open a tool to explore the file system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or a graphical file manager (like Files/Nautilus) is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Talon user directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file manager's address bar or the terminal's prompt/output shows the path containing '.talon/user'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the community command set directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The path '.talon/user/community' is visible in the file manager address bar or terminal prompt/output.
The full path ending in '.talon/user/community/core' is visible as the current working directory in the terminal or the active folder in the file manager.

task_completion.description: Verify the directory structure of the core community command files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents (files and subdirectories) of the 'core' directory are displayed on the screen, either as a list in the terminal (e.g., via 'ls' or 'tree' command) or as icons/list items in the file manager.

integrity.description: The agent is actually browsing the file system rather than viewing a text file or web page with the path written in it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is clearly a functional terminal or file manager, not a text editor or browser displaying mocked text.

integrity.description: The directory being viewed appears to be a genuine file system path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The path shown is an actual system path (e.g., /home/user/.talon/user/...) and not a manually typed string in an unrelated application.

integrity.description: The directory contents shown are from an actual system command or file manager view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The files and folders displayed are the result of a genuine command (like 'ls') or GUI rendering, not typed out manually by the agent.
